threshold_assessment.appropriateness: APPROPRIATE - 3 consecutive failures indicates a systemic issue requiring immediate attention

annotations.summary: Tempmemory ingestion has failed {{ $values.A }} times consecutively
annotations.description: The tempmemory ingestion runner has failed {{ $values.A }} times in a row. This may indicate a problem with the ingestion pipeline, Redis connectivity, or file system access.

threshold_assessment.rationale: Alert when ingestion takes longer than 5 minutes
threshold_assessment.appropriateness: APPROPRIATE - 5 minutes is a reasonable threshold for ingestion duration; tempmemory files are typically small and should process quickly

annotations.description: The tempmemory ingestion is taking longer than expected ({{ $values.A }} seconds). This may indicate performance issues with file parsing, Redis operations, or an unusually large backlog of files.

threshold_assessment.appropriateness: APPROPRIATE - 100 files represents significant backlog indicating ingestion cannot keep up with file creation rate

annotations.summary: Tempmemory ingestion backlog has {{ $values.A }} files (>100 threshold)
annotations.description: There are {{ $values.A }} files pending ingestion in the tempmemory backlog. This may indicate the ingestion runner is not keeping up with new file creation, or there may be a processing bottleneck.

threshold_assessment.rationale: Alert when no ingestion has run in over 2 hours
threshold_assessment.appropriateness: APPROPRIATE - 2 hours without ingestion indicates a complete pipeline failure (cron job not running, runner stuck, or connectivity issues)

annotations.summary: Tempmemory ingestion has not run in {{ $values.A }}s (>2h threshold)
annotations.description: No tempmemory ingestion has been recorded in the last {{ $values.A }} seconds. This may indicate the cron job is not running, the ingestion runner is stuck, or there is a connectivity issue with InfluxDB.

scenarios: Ingestion runner not keeping up with file creation

tempmemory-ingestion-stalled.description: Runbook for no recent run alerts
scenarios: Cron job not scheduled or disabled

high_latency.rationale: 5 minutes is reasonable for tempmemory processing

no_recent_run.rationale: 2 hours without ingestion is critical failure

validation_results.alert_definitions: PASS - 4 alerts defined with proper structure
validation_results.query_syntax: PASS - all queries use valid Flux syntax
validation_results.threshold_configuration: PASS - all thresholds are logically sound
validation_results.severity_levels: PASS - appropriate severity for each scenario
validation_results.runbook_documentation: PASS - all alerts have runbook URLs
validation_results.datasource_references: PASS - all alerts reference influxdb datasource
validation_results.evaluation_timing: PASS - evaluation intervals appropriate for alert type

issues_found.issue: Alerts reference governance bucket which does not exist
issues_found.impact: Alerts will fire with NoData state until bucket is created and populated
issues_found.recommendation: Create governance bucket and configure metrics emission before enabling alerts

issues_found.issue: Runbook URLs are documented but may not exist at those paths
issues_found.impact: On-call engineers may get 404 when clicking runbook links
issues_found.recommendation: Verify runbook pages exist at documented URLs or create them

recommendations.recommendation: Create the 'governance' InfluxDB bucket before enabling these alerts
recommendations.rationale: Alerts will not function correctly without the target bucket

recommendations.recommendation: Verify runbook pages exist at the documented URLs
recommendations.rationale: On-call engineers need working runbook links during incidents

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding alert routing rules in Alertmanager or Grafana OnCall
recommendations.rationale: Critical alerts should page on-call, warnings can go to Slack/email

recommendations.recommendation: Add alert testing procedure to CI/CD pipeline
recommendations.rationale: Ensure alerts fire correctly when conditions are met

alert_testing_procedure.description: Steps to test each alert fires correctly
steps: 1. Write test metric to governance bucket: governance.tempmemory.ingestion.consecutive_failures = 3
2. Wait for evaluation interval (1m) + for duration (2m) = 3 minutes
7. Repeat for backlog and no_recent_run alerts
8. Clean up test data from governance bucket

overall_status: PASS - All 4 alerts are correctly configured with appropriate thresholds and runbooks. Alerts will function once governance bucket is created.
